commands.repair_reason: Human review is the right lane because the remaining blocker is compatibility/session-history ownership, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff does not change dependencies or workflows and includes sensitive-media mirror safeguards. commands.review_summary:
This PR adds Gateway internal source-reply broadcasting and durable mirroring for chat surfaces, labels message-tool source replies as `internal-ui`, refactors assistant display helpers, and adds route, media, session-history, and TUI documentation coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for review purposes: source inspection shows current main lacks the internal-source final broadcaster and reports the source-reply tool payload as `webchat`, and the PR body includes a base negative control plus current-head live TUI proof. I did not rerun the live repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: Human review is the right lane because the remaining blocker is compatibility/session-history ownership, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix TUI-visible source replies from the message tool without turning `internal-ui` into a public deliverable channel.
- Keep the internal runtime route identity as `webchat`, but report message-tool source replies to the model/user as `channel: "internal-ui"` and `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`.
- Split source-reply broadcast/mirror logic into `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-internal-source-reply.ts`, assistant display projection into `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-assistant-content.ts`, and client surface identity into `src/gateway/protocol/client-info.ts`.
- Add a boundary test so internal UI source replies cannot regress back to a model-facing `webchat` channel label.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #38957

## Design Boundary

This PR intentionally does not create a new public channel, config key, CLI flag, provider policy, or external delivery path. `internal-ui` is an internal sink marker for message-tool source replies on OpenClaw-owned chat surfaces such as TUI, WebChat, Control UI, and native UI mode.

External channels such as Telegram, Slack, and Discord remain separate deliverable channels. Their messages are not made visible in WebChat/TUI by this change, and they do not inherit this internal UI source-reply sink.

The surface-risk check is the main reason the PR keeps `MessageActionRunResult.channel` as the selected internal runtime route while changing the source-reply result payload that the model sees. The model should not be told that it sent a user-visible reply to `webchat` when the active source chat was the TUI.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI-originated message-tool source replies render in the active TUI, and the tool result now identifies the internal UI sink instead of reporting a misleading `webchat` delivery channel to the model.

Real environment tested: Live local dev host with an isolated OpenClaw home, no `.openclaw/npm` plugin shadow in that proof home, no listener on port `18789`, a foreground gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, local bundled plugins from this worktree, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_BROWSER_CONTROL_SERVER=1`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CANVAS_HOST=1`, `OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (455729f)`, exact PR head `455729fff3b625b2282657ac2db080b74833f43f`, interactive TUI session `live-proof-20260520-0315`, and live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PROOF_HOME=<isolated temp OpenClaw home>

OPENCLAW_HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_BROWSER_CONTROL_SERVER=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CANVAS_HOST=1 \
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 \
OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR="$PWD/extensions" \
OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 \
OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_CATALOG_PATHS= \
OPENCLAW_PLUGINS_PATHS= \
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18789 --bind loopback --ws-log compact --force

OPENCLAW_HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \
node scripts/run-node.mjs tui --session live-proof-20260520-0315 --timeout-ms 180000
```

Then I submitted this prompt in the real interactive TUI PTY:

```text
LIVE_PROOF_455729fff3_INTERACTIVE Use the message tool to send exactly this visible reply to the current chat: TUI internal-ui source reply final 455729fff3 visible. Do not include any other final answer text.
```

Then I queried the transcript:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME="$PROOF_HOME" \
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call chat.history --json --params '{"sessionKey":"agent:main:live-proof-20260520-0315","limit":50}'
```

Evidence after fix: The active TUI rendered this standalone reply:

```text
TUI internal-ui source reply final 455729fff3 visible.
```

The `chat.history` response for session `agent:main:live-proof-20260520-0315` showed session id `90ae93a5-fef3-44c9-9769-1dd3581f0d7d`, the user nonce `LIVE_PROOF_455729fff3_INTERACTIVE`, provider/model `openai-codex` / `gpt-5.5`, a `message` tool call with `arguments.action: "send"` and `arguments.message: "TUI internal-ui source reply final 455729fff3 visible."`, and this tool result shape:

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "deliveryStatus": "sent",
  "channel": "internal-ui",
  "target": "current-run",
  "sourceReplyDeliveryMode": "message_tool_only",
  "sourceReplySink": "internal-ui",
  "sourceReply": {
    "text": "TUI internal-ui source reply final 455729fff3 visible."
  },
  "message": "TUI internal-ui source reply final 455729fff3 visible.",
  "dryRun": false
}
```

The transcript also persisted the matching `delivery-mirror` assistant message with idempotency key `0e7d14ff-4c6c-454a-96e1-a94649a702bf:internal-source-reply:0`.

Observed result after fix: The message-tool source reply was visible in the active TUI and the model-facing tool result now reports the internal UI sink, proving the fix covers both the user-visible failure and the misleading channel label.

What was not tested: Live Telegram/Slack/Discord delivery was not rerun for this final proof because this PR does not change their external delivery paths. The managed gateway and normal dev home were not used for the final proof because the isolated proof home avoided persisted plugin shadowing. `tui --message` did not dispatch during the final proof, so I used an interactive TUI PTY submission.

Before evidence: An earlier base negative control on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834` reproduced the failure shape: the model used the `message` tool, history showed the source reply was sent and persisted with `sourceReplySink: "internal-ui"`, but the active TUI returned to idle without rendering the standalone reply.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` (4 files, 158 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile "$OPENCLAW_TSBUILDINFO" --pretty false`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (clean after rebase; no actionable correctness issues found)
- Live interactive TUI/gateway proof on exact PR head `455729fff3b625b2282657ac2db080b74833f43f`, described above

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: yes
- Config/env changes: no
- Public SDK/API changes: no
- External channel behavior changes: no
target.author: rubencu
